rationale.R1.1: A review of this section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution claim and framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the progression from prior adaptive inference to the proposed router and claimed results is logically consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section reports benchmark results, so review centrally includes whether the stated performance claims are supported by those reported measurements.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact makes a first-work claim and compares against named related approaches, making novelty assessment central.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: Because the section positions itself against prior systems, review includes whether attribution and literature coverage are adequate.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer should disclose uncertainty where the artifact alone is insufficient to verify technical or empirical claims.
rationale.RX.2: The review should flag first, negligible-loss, and heavy-detector-quality claims if they exceed the evidence presented.
rationale.RX.3: The projection must stay within reviewing responsibilities and not execute edits or rewrite the section.
rationale.RX.4: The review should avoid endorsing unsupported claims that could mislead downstream readers or reviewers.
rationale.RX.5: Any assessment should remain traceable to specific claims and evidence present in the artifact.
